## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)

On May 21, 1927, Charles Lindbergh, a massive, incredibly daring American aviator, successfully concluded his monumental, deeply historic flight. Touching down at Le Bourget Field in Paris, France, he aggressively completed the absolute very first solo, nonstop transatlantic flight aboard his massive monoplane, the *Spirit of St. Louis*. His incredible, 33.5-hour journey over the massive, freezing Atlantic absolutely electrified the globe, fundamentally launching the massive, modern era of commercial aviation and making him a massive global hero.

On May 21, 1881, the massive, deeply influential, and incredibly vital American Red Cross was officially and aggressively founded by Clara Barton in Washington, D.C. Heavily inspired by her massive, heroic experiences nursing wounded soldiers during the brutal American Civil War and her subsequent massive exposure to the International Red Cross network in Europe, she established this massive humanitarian organization to aggressively provide absolute, indispensable disaster relief and emergency medical assistance during massive domestic and global crises.

On May 21, 1991, an incredibly horrific, massive, and deeply traumatic national tragedy aggressively shattered the Republic of India. The massive, devastating explosion killed Gandhi and 14 others, fundamentally altering the massive trajectory of Indian politics and deeply solidifying India's absolute, uncompromising stance against the militant Sri Lankan separatist group.
